name: Dr Taylor Joyal
focus: Fluvial geomorphology • Watershed hydrology • Post-wildfire flood impacts
bio: Dr. Taylor Joyal is an Associate Teaching Professor in the School of Earth and Sustainability at Northern Arizona University, where he has been a faculty member since 2014. He specializes in fluvial geomorphology and watershed hydrology, with particular expertise in watershed processes, river system dynamics in arid and tropical climates, and post-wildfire flood impacts. His research spans river channel dynamics and sediment transport, surface water–groundwater interactions, post-fire debris flows and watershed recovery, and the geomorphic response of rivers to dam removal and restoration. Field sites range from watersheds across the southwestern United States and post-fire study areas in the western US to the central highlands of Costa Rica. Dr. Joyal holds a Ph.D. in Water Resources Science and Management, an M.S. in Quaternary Sciences, and B.A. degrees in Geology and Anthropology, and he has authored 22+ publications with more than 560 citations. He is recognized for integrating field-based learning with research, teaching courses in environmental science, geomorphology, hydrology, and field methods, and collaborates broadly through Alexander Fremier's Lab and regional watershed and post-fire research networks.
